questions.question: What does System.out.printf() do differently from System.out.println()?
options: printf() automatically adds a newline at the end
printf() uses format specifiers to control how values are displayed
printf() can only print strings, not numbers
questions.answer: printf() uses format specifiers to control how values are displayed

questions.question: Which format specifier is used to print an integer with printf()?
questions.answer: %d

questions.question: What is the output of: System.out.printf("%f", 3.14);
Which format specifier prints a floating-point number rounded to 2 decimal places?
What does the format specifier %s represent?
questions.answer: A string

questions.question: What is the difference between %n and \n in a printf format string?
options: %n prints the number of characters written so far
%n is a platform-independent newline while \n always uses the Unix newline character
%n prints the letter n while \n prints a newline
questions.answer: %n is a platform-independent newline while \n always uses the Unix newline character

questions.question: Which format specifier is used to display a single character?
questions.answer: %c

questions.question: What is the output of: System.out.printf("%b", true);
questions.answer: true

questions.question: What does the format specifier %10d do?
options: Right-aligns the integer in a field 10 characters wide
Left-aligns the integer in a field 10 characters wide
Prints the integer with 10 decimal places
questions.answer: Right-aligns the integer in a field 10 characters wide

questions.question: What does the - flag do in %-15s?
options: Left-aligns the string in a field 15 characters wide
Right-aligns the string in a field 15 characters wide
questions.answer: Left-aligns the string in a field 15 characters wide

questions.question: What is the output of: System.out.printf("%05d", 42);
What is the key difference between System.out.printf() and String.format()?
options: printf() returns a String while String.format() prints to the console
String.format() returns a String while printf() prints to the console
String.format() does not support format specifiers
printf() can only format numbers, not strings
questions.answer: String.format() returns a String while printf() prints to the console

questions.question: Which of the following correctly uses String.format()?
questions.answer: String s = String.format("Hello, %s!", name);

questions.question: What does the escape sequence \t produce?
questions.answer: A tab character

questions.question: How do you print a literal backslash character in a Java string?
Which escape sequence allows you to include a double quote inside a string literal?
What is the output of: System.out.println("Line1\nLine2");
options: Line1 on one line and Line2 on the next line
questions.answer: Line1 on one line and Line2 on the next line

questions.question:
What does the following code print?

System.out.printf("%-15s %3d %8.2f%n", "Alice", 95, 92.50);
options: Alice followed by right-aligned 95 and right-aligned 92.50 with proper spacing
An error because you cannot mix format specifiers
questions.answer: Alice followed by right-aligned 95 and right-aligned 92.50 with proper spacing

questions.question: Why are escape sequences necessary in Java?
options: They make the code run faster
They allow you to represent special characters that cannot be typed directly in a string literal
They are required by the Java compiler for all strings
questions.answer: They allow you to represent special characters that cannot be typed directly in a string literal

questions.question: What is the output of: System.out.printf("%,.2f", 1234.5);
Which of the following correctly prints the path C:\Users\Documents in Java?
questions.answer: System.out.println("C:\\Users\\Documents");

questions.question: What happens if the number of format specifiers in printf does not match the number of arguments?
options: Default values are used for missing arguments
questions.answer: A runtime exception is thrown

questions.question: When is String.format() more useful than printf()?
options: When you want to print directly to the console
When you need to store the formatted result in a variable for later use
When you are formatting integers only
questions.answer: When you need to store the formatted result in a variable for later use

questions.question: What does the escape sequence \' represent in Java?
options: An apostrophe that terminates the string
A backslash followed by a quote
questions.answer: A literal single quote

questions.question: What is the output of: System.out.printf("%10.2f", 3.14159);
options: 3.14 with leading spaces to fill a 10-character-wide field
questions.answer: 3.14 with leading spaces to fill a 10-character-wide field
